codeReview.summary: The fix correctly adds shell quoting for createTag() and introduces stderr redaction for AuthError, but it does not fully resolve the prior blocking issues: checkoutRef() now invokes git's pathspec form instead of checking out refs, and the redaction regex still leaks credential suffixes when a password contains '@'.

issues.description: checkoutRef() now runs `git checkout -- ${shellQuote(ref)}`. In git, inserting `--` here switches the command from branch/tag/SHA checkout mode into pathspec mode, so ordinary refs stop working. A local repro against a temp repo showed `git checkout main` exiting 0 with `Already on 'main'`, while `git checkout -- main` exited 1 with `error: pathspec 'main' did not match any file(s) known to git`. The string-assertion updates in packages/git-ops/test/git-ops.test.ts:712 and :753 therefore bless a broken command shape rather than verifying the advertised checkout behavior.

redactCredentials() only replaces up to the first `@` in the credential segment. For example, `https://admin:p@ssw0rd@git.example.com/repo.git` is transformed into `https://<redacted>@ssw0rd@git.example.com/repo.git`, which still leaks part of the password. That means the prior AuthError credential-exposure failure is only partially fixed. The new test at packages/git-ops/test/security.test.ts:398-403 checks that the full original token string disappears, but it does not assert that fragments such as `ssw0rd` are removed too.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill still requires manual SSH verification for SSH-related work, but this fix handoff records only local automated commands while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` remains true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend-worker SKILL.md step 6 ('Manual Verification') requires SSH checks such as `ssh shuvtest ...`. The handoff file /home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/handoffs/2026-03-15T19-50-04-510Z__fix-git-ops-security__dd524d3d-344a-4aaf-b684-08626624bc1f.json lists only `npm test`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run lint` under verification.commandsRun, yet marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared guidance does not currently capture that shell-safety fixes for git command construction need a real git-behavior check, not just mock SSH command-string assertions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/git-ops/test/git-ops.test.ts:712 and :753 plus packages/git-ops/test/security.test.ts assert only the emitted command strings. A temp-repo git repro showed the semantic mismatch directly: `git checkout main` succeeded, while `git checkout -- main` failed as a pathspec. A note in shared guidance/library about validating git CLI semantics with an isolated local repo would have caught this regression.

summary: Reviewed the fix handoff, transcript skeleton, prior failed reviews, and commit 1905ed4. createTag() is now quoted safely, but checkoutRef() became functionally incorrect by switching to git's pathspec form, and AuthError redaction still leaks password suffixes for credentials containing '@', so the fix does not yet clear the prior blocking issues.
